title: Small Benchmark Story
description: Show v0->v1 relation gain, repair effect, and mock/external contrast on core indoor scenes.

steps.focus: Calibration improves relation structure with minimal visual disruption.
talking_points: Start with v0 vs v1 to show direct calibration effect before repair.
Highlight relation and label changes first, then object counts.

steps.focus: Repair stage resolves residual structural violations.
talking_points: Use this step to explain calibration-only vs calibration+repair roles.
Emphasize that repair is explicit, inspectable post-processing.

steps.focus: Compare controlled internal generator against external backend behavior.
talking_points: Mention that both settings share the same calibration path.
Point out semantic drift or relation sparsity on external outputs if present.

steps.focus: Demonstrate effect of skipping calibration entirely.
talking_points: Frame this as an uncertainty-decision baseline.
Use relation and label evidence to discuss actionability impact.

steps.focus: Close with another v0->v1 scene to reinforce consistency.
talking_points: Summarize what improved and what still needs external-path refinement.

title: Public Subset Relation Walkthrough
description: Auto-build steps from first public scenes for v0->v1 and mock->external relation comparison.

strategy.focus: Public-scene robustness and relation propagation under ambiguity.

title: Mock vs External Focus
description: Cycle through scenes comparing mock generator and external SpatialLM outputs.

strategy.focus: Controlled-vs-real backend differences in objects, relations, and language artifacts.
